codeReview.summary: The implementation correctly wires daemon terminal events (completed/failed/stopped) to broadcast delivery-loop refetch messages. The code is well-structured, follows existing patterns, and properly prevents broadcast spam by only emitting when the terminal dispatch status was actually updated.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation correctly uses the existing publishBroadcastUserMessage pattern from @terragon/shared/broadcast-server, consistent with other broadcast usages in handle-daemon-event.ts.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: apps/www/src/app/api/daemon-event/route.ts:1389-1423 - Imports and uses publishBroadcastUserMessage with proper user message envelope structure matching existing patterns.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The didUpdateTerminalDispatchStatus flag pattern is a clean way to track material state changes and prevent duplicate broadcasts. This follows the spirit of AGENTS.md's event-driven delivery-loop guidance.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: apps/www/src/app/api/daemon-event/route.ts:1382 - Flag initialized to false, set to true only when persistDaemonTerminalDispatchStatus promise resolves, then checked before broadcast.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Error handling for broadcast failures uses .catch() with console.warn, which is non-blocking and follows the same pattern as other non-critical operations in the codebase.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: apps/www/src/app/api/daemon-event/route.ts:1420-1427 - Broadcast failure is logged but doesn't prevent the terminal ack response from being sent.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The broadcast service (apps/broadcast on port 3002) is already documented in AGENTS.md and doesn't require additional configuration for this feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md mentions '3002: WebSocket broadcast service (apps/broadcast)' - the delivery-loop refetch uses the existing publishBroadcastUserMessage infrastructure.

summary: Feature m2-wire-daemon-events-to-broadcast is well-implemented and passes review. The code correctly triggers delivery-loop refetch broadcasts on terminal daemon events while preventing spam through the didUpdateTerminalDispatchStatus flag. All 47 daemon-event tests pass, TypeScript compiles cleanly, and the implementation follows established codebase patterns.
